articles.title: RUBBER-Japan rubber futures rise as oil rebounds, weak yen supports prices
articles.description:
Japanese rubber futures edged higher on
Monday, supported by a rebound in oil prices and a near 40-year
low in the yen, though improved supply capped gains.
* The Osaka Exchange rubber...

articles.title: Fuel excise cuts likely to be extended and tapered off over coming months
articles.description: At present, the reductions in excise of 32 cent a litre on diesel and 27 cent for petrol are due to expire at the end of July.

articles.title: Oil climbs following renewed US, Iran strikes in Middle East
articles.description: SINGAPORE: Oil prices rose on Monday following days of tit-for-tat strikes by the U.S. and Iran that underscored the fragility of their interim peace deal and again slowed energy shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Middle East Energy Export Resilience Amid Rising Tensions
articles.description: Despite recent tensions and attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, Middle Eastern producers continue to export oil and LNG. Iran accelerates exports after a U.S. sanctions waiver, while Saudi Arabia and UAE maintain shipments. This ongoing export activity is impacting global oil prices, causing fluctuations.

articles.title: Middle East Oil and LNG Exports Forge On Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
articles.description: Middle East producers persist with oil and LNG shipments despite rising tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. Recent U.S.-Iran hostilities, including ship attacks, strained their interim peace agreement. Still, oil loadings continue from key terminals, impacting global oil prices, while LNG exports proceed from Qatar and UAE.

articles.title: Asian shares are mixed as tech stocks fall in Japan and South Korea
articles.description: HONG KONG, June 29: Asian markets were mixed on Monday as selling of artificial intelligence-related shares pulled benchmarks in Japan and South Korea lower, though gains for other stocks helped offset those losses. US futures advanced and oil prices gained,...

articles.title: Euro Zone Bond Yields Steady as Oil Prices Ease Inflation Fears
articles.description: Euro zone bond yields remain low amid easing inflation concerns due to declining oil prices, significantly impacting investors and central banking strategies at the ECB's Sintra Forum. Key figures such as Christine Lagarde and Kevin Warsh are expected to shed light on economic policy developments. Inflation data from major economies will be pivotal this week.

articles.title: Energy bills rise this week but experts predict no further increase in October
articles.description: The Ofgem price cap increases to £1,862 a year from July 1, but falling wholesale gas prices have improved forecasts for household bills this autumn.

articles.title: India bonds edge up, supported by steady oil prices, halt to US-Iran strikes
articles.description: Indian government bonds saw an early rise on Monday, buoyed by strong foreign investment and stable oil prices, which helped offset geopolitical tensions. Despite recent U.S.-Iran strikes, a diplomatic resolution and a dip in crude oil prices eased inflation concerns. This positive sentiment, coupled with RBI incentives and tax cuts, has attracted significant foreign inflows into government bonds, boosting market confidence.

articles.title: India Reclaims 5th Spot In Global Market Cap Rankings As AI-Fuelled Asia Selloff Hits Taiwan, South Korea
articles.description: India has climbed back to fifth place in global market capitalisation rankings as Taiwan and South Korea slip amid profit booking in AI and semiconductor stocks. India outperformed global peers in June, supported by lower crude oil prices, improved valuations, and steady foreign institutional investor inflows
